questions.question: Which of the following describes 'Heterosis' in animal breeding?

options.A: The superior performance of hybrid progeny over both parental strains
options.B: The decline in fertility due to continuous inbreeding
options.C: The emergence of entirely new species from two distinct genera
options.D: The uniform characteristics observed in a pure-line

questions.question: In poultry management, which of the following practices is essential to prevent the spread of devastating diseases like Ranikhet?

options.A: Maintaining strict hygiene, proper ventilation, and regular vaccination
options.B: Feeding the birds exclusively with high-protein oceanic fish
options.C: Keeping the birds isolated in completely dark environments
options.D: Inbreeding the birds continuously to develop natural resistance

questions.question: The pacemaker of the human heart is: (Exam form 740)
questions.solution: By definition, Sino-atrial node (SA Node) performs this role.

questions.question: Which step directly follows the administration of FSH-like hormones to the genetic mother in the MOET procedure?

options.A: Superovulation and subsequent mating with an elite bull (or AI)
options.B: Immediate surgical removal of the unfertilized eggs
options.C: Implantation of the eggs into the surrogate mother

questions.question: Which of the following marine fishes is widely consumed and forms a major part of India's coastal economy?
Which of the following is NOT an objective of cross-breeding in animal husbandry?

options.B: Combining the desirable qualities of two different breeds
options.C: Introducing new genetic variations into a herd

questions.question: Which part of the brain controls balance and posture? (Exam form 299)
questions.solution: By definition, Cerebellum performs this role.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding honeybees. Which one is true?

options.A: The queen bee is fertile, while worker bees are sterile females.
options.B: The drone bee is a sterile male.
options.C: Worker bees are responsible only for reproduction.
options.D: The queen bee gathers nectar and produces wax.

questions.question: The joint between Atlas and Axis is a: (Exam form 252)
questions.solution: By definition, Pivot joint performs this role.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of storing semen in Artificial Insemination?

options.A: It allows the semen to be used long after the donor male is dead or transported far away.
options.B: It immediately cures sterility in the recipient female.
options.D: It guarantees that the resulting offspring will be exclusively female.

questions.question: If a breeder wishes to develop a high-yielding, disease-resistant variety of cattle quickly, they should ideally employ a combination of which techniques?

options.A: Artificial Insemination and Multiple Ovulation Embryo Transfer (MOET)

questions.question: The joint between Atlas and Axis is a: (Exam form 706)
questions.solution: By definition, Pivot joint performs this role.

questions.question: Interspecific hybridization often produces offspring that are robust but sterile. Which of the following is the most classic example?
Which of the following practices does NOT fall under 'Animal Husbandry'?
Which of the following correctly pairs an Indian freshwater fish with its habitat?
What happens if continuous inbreeding is maintained over 5-6 generations in cattle?

options.A: Inbreeding depression occurs, causing reduced fertility and milk yield.
options.B: The herd becomes fully resistant to all diseases.
options.D: The offspring mutate into a new breed entirely.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most abundant component of a standard, well-formulated feed for dairy cattle?
When developing the Hisardale sheep breed, what was the primary breeding method used?
What is the primary factor driving the success of a dairy farm's overall milk production?

options.A: The genetic quality and breed of the cattle
options.B: The geographical location of the farm
options.D: The temperature of the milking parlor

questions.question: The structural and functional unit of kidney is: (Exam form 411)
questions.solution: By definition, Nephron performs this role.
